ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ka yɛ yeli, ‘Soba na zaa naŋ pɔ a Naaŋmen bagmaalkuuri, pɔraa zaa ba dɔgle o zu, kyɛ soba na zaa naŋ pɔ ne a kyɔɔtaare boma naŋ biŋ a Naaŋmen bagmaalkuuri zu, a pɔraa na do la o soba.’
translated: And he said, 'Whoever keeps God's commandments, all people will follow him, but whoever keeps things that are against God's commandments, the people will abandon him.

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, a yɛlɛ ama zaa na do la a zenɛ noba zu.” Yeezu Naŋ Koŋ A Gyɛruzɛlɛm Teŋɛ Yɛlɛ (Luk
translated: Truly I tell you, all these things will happen to this generation.” Jesus Weeps Over the City of Jerusalem (Luke

text: Noba yaga na zagre la a Naaŋmen yɛlɛ a lɛ saŋ, a yire taa poe, kyɛ kyiire taa.
translated: When people do good with God's word, their home is good, and their outside life is also good.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Kyɛ ka ba naŋ da ba ŋmaa a bebie ama kyɛlɛɛ bare, nensaala zaa da koŋ nyɛ faabo. Kyɛ a neŋkaa-irri zuiŋ, ba na ŋmaa la a bebie mine bare.
translated: But if they were to have a child, and not just any child, not everyone would receive a blessing. But concerning the woman's womb, they would also be able to have a child.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Aŋa a bebie na poɔ a kɔmpiiri naŋ da ba wa sɛre, ka noba da dire kyɛ nyuuro, ka dɔbɔ derɛ pɔgeba, ka pɔgeba meŋ kulo serebɛ, a te ta daare na a Nowa naŋ kpɛ a gbori,
translated: And the men and women who had no shame at all, and people were eating and drinking, and men were marrying women, and women themselves were also marrying, until the day that Noah entered the ark,

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: a gbaŋgbaa ŋa daana na uri wa la daare na o naŋ ba nare ane gogo na o naŋ ba baŋ.
translated: A bat, which was its own master, came into the day, and it had no name or surname, and it had no clan.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “A manne seŋ ŋa dɔɔ kaŋ naŋ boɔrɔ ka o tu sori, a boɔle o gbaŋgbaare a de o boma ko ba ka ba kaara;
translated: He went to his friend who needed help, he called his wife and gave them something for them to eat.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: A nemboorɔ zaa na laŋ la taa O niŋesogɔ, ka O wɛle ba taa, aŋa pekyeenɛ naŋ maŋ wɛle o peere ne a boore lɛ.
translated: All people will gather at His right hand, and He will separate them, like a shepherd who separates his sheep from the goats.

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Bonso a Pilato da nyɛɛ la ka nyuuri la da so ka ba nyɔge a Yeezu wa tere.
translated: But Pilate saw that the crowd was becoming a riot and they were insisting on killing Jesus.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: A Yeezu da are la a gɔbena niŋesogɔ, ka a gɔbena soore O, “Fõõ la a Gyuumine Naa be?” Ka a Yeezu yeli ko o, “Fõõ la yeli a lɛ.”
translated: Jesus was standing among the guards, and the guards asked Him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” And Jesus said to them, “You have said it.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: A Pilato naŋ da wa nyɛ ka o ba taa lɛ zaa o naŋ na e, kyɛ ka dɔɔne da piili ka o kpɛ a noba poɔ, O da de la koɔ̃ pɛge o nuuri a noba niŋesogɔ, a yele, “N ba baŋ yelzaa a dɔɔ ŋa kũũ eŋɛ; yɛ taa yɛ yɛlɛ.”
translated: When Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing, but that a riot was starting and spreading among the people, he took water and washed his hands before the people, and said, "I am innocent of this man's blood; you see to it yourselves.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Lɛ la ka a noba zaa pabo laŋ yeli, “Ka a Yeezu zẽẽ do te ne te biiri.”
translated: And so all the people gathered and said, “Let Jesus sit with us and our children.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.

translated: Joseph's father was a carpenter, and he also taught Jesus.

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.

translated: He said, "Neŋkpoŋ, our ancestors said to his descendants, who had told O vore, 'I will bring you back from the grave after three days.

translated: And then the angel said to the women, "Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.

translated: Saamon and his friend went to his house.

text: A pɛnnoo bebiri kaŋ daare, Yeezu da kyɛnɛ la a te tu kyi weɛ poɔ. Ba naŋ da kyɛnɛ gɛrɛ, a O potuuribo da piili ŋmaara la a kazuri mine.
translated: On the day of the Passover feast, Jesus was going to eat the Passover. As they were going, his disciples began to pluck the ears of grain.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: A noba da kaara la ka ba nyɛ ka a Yeezu na saŋ la a dɔɔ ŋa a pɛnnoo bebiri daare, ka ba na toɔ̃ nyɛ saanaa tuo O.
translated: The people were amazed when they saw Jesus walking on the water in the middle of the night, and they couldn't see anyone else.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Ka naaloŋ wa kyataa a leɛ zɔɔrɔ ne o meŋɛ, a naaloŋ na na le la.
translated: If a person comes and sits and makes himself a stranger by his own actions, that person will remain a stranger.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ka O yeli sɔge ba, “Nembuo la N ma ane N yɔɔmine?”
translated: And he asked them, "Who are my mother and my brothers?

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: A bombie mine meŋ da le la pikubo poɔ, zie naŋ ba taa tɛne yaga, ka a bombie buli wieouŋ zaa, a tɛne naŋ ba e zuluŋ zuiŋ.
translated: A bombie mine that was buried in the ground, in a place where the ground was not deep enough, and the bombie exploded completely, because the ground was not sufficiently deep.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Kyɛ ba naŋ ba tɛ nyaga zuiŋ, ba maŋ di la kannyiri belaa lɛ, kyɛ ka yelwonaa bee dɔgroŋ wa pɔge ba a Naaŋmen yelbiri ba naŋ de zuiŋ, ba maŋ faa le la wieouŋ zaa.
translated: And when they become human, they will eat only a little food, and if a wise person or a prophet gives them the word of God that they have, they will receive all the power.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: A boreborɔ maŋ bore la a Naaŋmen yelbiri.
translated: A fool does not understand God's word.

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Ka a Yeezu yeli ko o, “N pɔgeyaa, a fo yeldeebo saŋ fo la, gɛrɛ ne emmaaroŋ, fo nyɛ la sammo.”
translated: And Jesus said to her, "My woman, if your husband is with you, be careful with the truth, you are a prostitute.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: O naŋ da wa nyɛ a Yeezu ka O be tɔɔre zaa, o da zo te le vɔgle la muro O niŋesogɔ;
translated: He had come to see Jesus, and He was in great distress, he was afraid to ask about His suffering.

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.

translated: Isn't this the carpenter, Mary's son? And aren't his brothers James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon? And aren't all his sisters here with us as well?” So they took offense at him.

translated: Then the Pharisees and the teachers of the law asked Jesus, “Why do your disciples not follow the tradition of the elders but eat with unwashed hands?

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Yɛ zagre la a Naaŋmen nɔɛ bare kyɛ nyɔge nensaala wuluu taa kpeɛŋaa.”
translated: We are praying to God that power may be removed from humanity.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
